title: Enhance fuse-overlayfs instructions.
body:
The `fuse-overlayfs` package provided by Ubuntu up to 19.10,  is not recent enough and causes errors on `buildah commit`, for instance.

Adjust the rootless tutorial to point this out and to provide more detailed instructions on how to obtain `fuse-overlayfs` and configure it for use by libpod.

comments.body:
Hi @leorochael. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
By the way, I wonder if project kubic could include `fuse-overlayfs` on their ubuntu packaging...

> By the way, I wonder if project kubic could include `fuse-overlayfs` on their ubuntu packaging...

@lsm5 PTAL. https://build.opensuse.org/project/monitor/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable?arch_x86_64=1&blocked=1&broken=1&building=1&defaults=0&deleting=1&dispatching=1&failed=1&finished=1&locked=1&repo_xUbuntu_19_10=1&scheduled=1&signing=1&succeeded=1&unresolvable=1 doesn't list fuse-overlayfs for 19.10

> > By the way, I wonder if project kubic could include `fuse-overlayfs` on their ubuntu packaging...
> 
> @lsm5 PTAL. https://build.opensuse.org/project/monitor/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable?arch_x86_64=1&blocked=1&broken=1&building=1&defaults=0&deleting=1&dispatching=1&failed=1&finished=1&locked=1&repo_xUbuntu_19_10=1&scheduled=1&signing=1&succeeded=1&unresolvable=1 doesn't list fuse-overlayfs for 19.10

hmm, fuse-overlayfs is present on debian sid, I'll try and rebuild that for ubuntu.
I'd say stick with 0.76 if that's a released stable version and will work going forward.
@lsm5, would be nice if there was a version of `fuse-overlayfs` pre-packaged for Ubuntu 18.04 as well, since it's the current LTS and lots of Ubuntu derivatives are based on it.

> @lsm5, would be nice if there was a version of `fuse-overlayfs` pre-packaged for Ubuntu 18.04 as well, since it's the current LTS and lots of Ubuntu derivatives are based on it.

ya, i'll try and send one to obs now

available for ubuntu and debian now: https://build.opensuse.org/package/show/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable/fuse-overlayfs .. RE: 18.04 seems like the build env can't find some debhelper packages. I'll check with OBS people tomorrow about this and update this thread. HTH.
Since we now have the correct version available for Ubuntu, do we still need this PR?

> Since we now have the correct version available for Ubuntu, do we still need this PR?

let's wait till I hear from OBS guys about 18.04 build setup.

So, fuse3 (libfuse3-dev is a dep for fuse-overlayfs) isn't available for ubuntu 18.04 and building it appears to be non-trivial. I hope we can go ahead with this PR and not worry about 18.04 since a new LTS should be coming out in 2 months.

But in case you have buildable sources for fuse3 on 18.04, I can add them to OBS. Let me know..
